command_surface.surface_model: TigerKit exposes Claude Code plugin commands only; commands/*.md and .claude-plugin/plugin.json own /tk:* contracts. Active command surface is /tk:gap, /tk:route, /tk:reflect, /tk:learn, /tk:ui-diff, /tk:grill, /tk:prototype, /tk:arch-review, /tk:merge-conflict, /tk:handoff, /tk:to-prd, and /tk:to-issues.
command_surface.plugin_version_policy: Authoritative version is .claude-plugin/plugin.json; evals must not hard-code a concrete version literal.

evals.name: plugin-version-and-command-manifest-gap-route-reflect-ui-diff-plus-phase1-and-conditional
evals.prompt: Evaluate TigerKit active command surface and plugin packaging. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should list /tk:gap, /tk:route, /tk:reflect, /tk:learn, /tk:ui-diff, /tk:grill, /tk:prototype, /tk:arch-review, /tk:merge-conflict, /tk:handoff, /tk:to-prd, and /tk:to-issues as the active commands. Should state /tk:learn is a source-to-skill surface with preview-default and skill-only write boundary. Should state /tk:route is a decision/brainstorming surface, not a runner or autopilot. Should state /tk:reflect is the durable promotion router with repo-local/user-global default apply and explicit skill materialize mode, /tk:grill is a questioning surface, /tk:prototype is a throwaway validation surface, /tk:arch-review is a report-only architecture review surface, /tk:merge-conflict is a conflict-resolution surface, /tk:handoff is a repo-local handoff surface, /tk:to-prd is a draft-only PRD surface, and /tk:to-issues is a draft-only vertical-slice issue surface.
expectations: States /tk:reflect has explicit skill materialize mode
States /tk:arch-review is report-only architecture review surface
States /tk:to-issues is draft-only vertical-slice issue surface

evals.name: gap-is-sot-first-one-shot-analysis
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should treat /tk:gap as a one-shot comparison between source of truth and current implementation. Should say that if SoT exists, gap should be considered before implementation. If SoT does not exist, it should suggest providing one. If the user explicitly wants direct execution, it may proceed without gap while keeping assumptions explicit. It should classify missing, mismatch, overbuilt, and ambiguous findings. It should keep source conflicts ambiguous when precedence is not confirmed and should distinguish current-evidence strength instead of treating plans as implementation proof.
expectations: Allows direct execution when user explicitly wants it
Keeps source precedence conflicts ambiguous when not confirmed
Distinguishes current evidence type or strength
Does not treat plan-only evidence as implementation proof

evals.name: gap-does-not-generate-workflow
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap non-goals. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not generate a sealed workflow, workflow hash, launch state, autopilot policy, runner/advisor flow, or external side-effect receipt. It may compare current implementation evidence, but it should not expand into a supervisory workflow.
expectations: Does not mention advisor runner as active

evals.name: reflect-default-apply-canonical-targets
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect promotion router behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should define /tk:reflect as a safe durable promotion router that extracts reusable learning from session result and feedback, classifies it into canonical targets, and defaults repo-local guidance to apply-enabled with --apply=false as preview-only opt-out. Canonical target enum must be exactly repo-local, repo-shared, user-global, skill, hook, command, agent, discard. PROFILE.md, automation, hookify, and hook / hookify must not be target names. repo-local and user-global are the default-apply targets. skill is not default apply; it becomes writable only with explicit target skill plus explicit apply. repo-shared, hook, command, and agent remain suggest-only from reflect. discard implies action discard with no path or write. TigerKit must not write, mirror, or back up Claude Code auto memory.
expectations: Default repo-local reflect behavior is apply-enabled
Canonical target enum exactly repo-local repo-shared user-global skill hook command agent discard
PROFILE.md is not a promotion target
automation is not a target name
hookify is not a target name
repo-local and user-global are the default-apply targets
user-global can be direct-apply on supported hosts
skill requires explicit apply to write
discard has action discard and no path or write
hook command agent changes not auto applied
changed paths printed when files are written
TigerKit does not write mirror or back up Claude Code auto memory

evals.name: reflect-repo-local-default-apply-or-preview-opt-out
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect default behavior for a repo-local candidate. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should default repo-local guidance to apply-enabled when a candidate is eligible. With --apply=false it should produce a repo-local candidate as preview_only and must not claim Applied. If a candidate is outside eligibility in apply mode, it should use candidate_not_eligible; if no candidates are eligible, it should use no_eligible_candidates. Exact apply_plan should live in the ledger rather than requiring stdout to print it.
expectations: Default repo-local reflect behavior is apply-enabled
repo-local candidate can be preview_only when --apply=false
Does not claim Applied in preview mode
uses candidate_not_eligible for ineligible apply candidate
uses no_eligible_candidates when no candidate can be applied

evals.name: reflect-eligible-repo-local-apply
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect --apply=true for an eligible repo-local candidate. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should allow writing only <git-root>/CLAUDE.local.md when the repo-local candidate is eligible. It should discover the git root from invocation_cwd, use root-relative operand CLAUDE.local.md, verify untracked and ignored status, reject symlinks and paths outside repo, produce exact apply_plan with base/result hashes and planned result bytes hash, write via same-directory temp file and atomic replace, verify result bytes, and report the changed path.
expectations: normalized path remains inside git root
apply plan includes exact apply set
apply plan includes base_state base_sha256 result_sha256 planned result bytes hash
apply plan includes exact unified diff
writes using same-directory temp file and atomic replace
receipt shows direct-apply vs explicit skill vs suggest-only boundary

evals.name: reflect-tracked-claude-local-rejection
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect --apply=true when <git-root>/CLAUDE.local.md is tracked. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should run git -C <git-root> ls-files --error-unmatch -- CLAUDE.local.md. Exit 0 means the file is tracked and must be rejected with reason_code tracked_local_file. Exit 1 means untracked and may continue. Other status must be tracked_check_error. A tracked CLAUDE.local.md candidate must not be applied.

evals.name: reflect-not-ignored-local-file-rejection
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect --apply=true when CLAUDE.local.md is not ignored. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should run git -C <git-root> check-ignore -q --no-index -- CLAUDE.local.md. Exit 0 means ignored and may continue. Exit 1 means not_ignored and must reject. Exit 128 or other status must be ignore_check_error. not_ignored may propose an ignore entry but must not edit .gitignore, .git/info/exclude, or global exclude.

evals.name: reflect-non-git-rejection
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect --apply=true outside a Git worktree. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should run git -C <invocation_cwd> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree. If exit status is not 0, repo-local apply must be rejected with reason_code not_git_worktree. It should produce no apply plan, write no files, and must not fall back to workspace-root writes.

evals.name: reflect-git-root-resolution-and-path-safety
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect git root resolution, nested cwd, paths with spaces, symlink, and path normalization. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should run git -C <invocation_cwd> rev-parse --show-toplevel to get git root, including nested cwd and paths with spaces. Command invocation must use argument vector, not shell string concatenation. Normalized <git-root>/CLAUDE.local.md must remain inside the git root. lstat symlink check must reject symlink_target before following a target. Root resolution failure should use reason_code git_root_resolution_error and path escape should use path_outside_repo.
expectations: paths with spaces supported by argument vector
normalized path must remain inside git root

evals.name: reflect-user-global-apply-on-supported-hosts
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect --target user-global --apply=true. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should allow user-global candidates to write on hosts with a writable user-global guidance surface. For Claude Code style hosts this can be ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md or ~/.claude/rules/<rule-name>/CLAUDE.md. For agents that do not use CLAUDE.md, it should resolve to a host-native user-global guidance surface when available; otherwise the candidate should remain suggest_only with a precise reason. It must not substitute auto memory or generated state for the writable target.
expectations: user-global can write on supported hosts
non-CLAUDE host may use host-native user-global surface
does not substitute auto memory for target
does not use generated state as target

evals.name: reflect-simple-role-fact-discard-no-profile
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect on a simple transient role fact such as 'I was acting as release helper in this session'. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should discard a simple role fact when it is not reusable durable guidance. It must not create or propose PROFILE.md as a promotion target. Discard candidate should use target discard, action discard, path NONE, and no write.
expectations: simple role fact can be discarded
PROFILE.md is not a promotion target

evals.name: reflect-repo-canonical-source-suggest-only
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect when a learning belongs in a repo canonical shared source. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should classify repo-wide durable team guidance as repo-shared and suggest-only. It may recommend a specific repo shared surface for human review, but it must not edit CLAUDE.md or shared docs automatically.
expectations: does not edit shared docs automatically
human review required before shared source change

evals.name: reflect-branch-specific-workaround-discard-session-local
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect on a branch-specific workaround discovered during a one-off implementation. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should discard branch-specific one-off or workaround guidance instead of promoting it to durable repo/user guidance. Candidate should use target discard, action discard, path NONE, and reason_code session_local. It must not use session-local status.

evals.name: reflect-target-specific-skill-hook-command-agent-proposals
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect classification for target-specific procedural proposals. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should keep skill, hook, command, and agent proposals separate. Skill candidates are suggest-only reusable procedures. Hook candidates include rationale, trigger, action, and why suggest-only. Command candidates include intent, arguments, and when better than skill. Agent candidates include role boundary, responsibility, and when better than command. None of these are installed, generated, dispatched, or activated by reflect.
expectations: hook proposal includes rationale trigger action why suggest-only
command proposal includes intent arguments when better than skill
agent proposal includes role boundary responsibility when better than command

evals.name: reflect-discard-invariant
evals.prompt: Evaluate discard invariants in /tk:reflect output. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Every discard item must remain target discard with action discard, path NONE, and no write. Discard items must not appear in apply_set and must not become preview_only or suggest_only. Low-confidence, sensitive, duplicate, already documented, or branch-specific items should be discarded rather than promoted.

evals.name: reflect-multi-candidate-all-or-nothing-patch
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect --apply=true with multiple eligible repo-local candidates. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should combine all eligible repo-local candidates into one planned CLAUDE.local.md result bytes and exact unified diff. The apply_set must be exact and immutable. Apply must be all-or-nothing; partial candidate application is forbidden. If any included candidate or target fails eligibility/recheck/verification, no candidate may be reported Applied.
expectations: multiple eligible candidates combine into one planned result bytes
no candidate reported Applied after failure

evals.name: reflect-stale-apply-plan
evals.prompt: Evaluate stale apply plan handling in /tk:reflect --apply=true. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should treat apply plans as current invocation only. Before writing, it must recheck base_state and base_sha256. If target bytes/state changed after planning or the plan came from a prior invocation, apply must abort with reason_code stale_apply_plan, write no files, and report no candidates Applied.
expectations: write no files on stale plan
Applied candidates NONE on stale plan

evals.name: reflect-post-write-verification-rollback-success
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect post-write verification failure with successful rollback. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: If post-write target bytes do not match result_sha256, reflect should report reason_code apply_verification_failed, capture failed result bytes/hash, report Applied candidates: NONE, rollback safely to original bytes or original absence, and if rollback succeeds output rollback: succeeded and Changed paths: NONE.
expectations: rollback restores original bytes or original absence
Changed paths NONE after rollback success
verification failure and rollback success are distinct receipt states

evals.name: reflect-post-write-verification-rollback-failure
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect post-write verification failure with rollback failure or external change. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: If verification fails and rollback precheck detects an external change, or rollback cannot restore original bytes/absence safely, reflect should report reason_code rollback_failed, Applied candidates: NONE, rollback: failed, changed path <git-root>/CLAUDE.local.md, and required manual inspection before rerun.
expectations: verification failure and rollback failure are distinct receipt states

evals.name: reflect-visible-auto-memory-duplicate
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect when visible Claude Code auto memory already contains the same reusable guidance. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: If direct visible evidence shows an existing auto memory duplicate, reflect should set duplicate_status confirmed and normally discard or avoid re-promoting the same content. TigerKit must not write, mirror, or back up Claude Code auto memory.
expectations: visible duplicate evidence sets duplicate_status confirmed
duplicate can be discarded or not re-promoted
TigerKit does not write Claude Code auto memory
TigerKit does not mirror Claude Code auto memory
TigerKit does not back up Claude Code auto memory

evals.name: reflect-unobserved-auto-memory-duplicate-status-unknown
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect when auto memory duplicate evidence was not observed. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: If auto memory was not inspected or visible evidence is absent, reflect should set duplicate_status unknown, not duplicate_status not_observed. It must not claim a duplicate was absent merely because it did not inspect auto memory.
expectations: does not infer absence from lack of inspection

evals.name: route-is-decision-brainstorming-surface
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:route behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should treat /tk:route as an explicit-trigger decision/brainstorming surface that compares implementation routes such as direct, subagent-driven, and goal-driven. It must not run package scripts, tests, builds, network, runtime execute, runner/autopilot, approval lifecycle, or mutate source tree/.claude/tigerkit. It should be willing to stop at need-sot or decision when route choice is premature. When goal-driven is chosen and the host supports /goal, it may include a ready-to-run /goal recommendation. It must not suggest off-contract wording hacks to sneak around rejections.
expectations: Can return need-sot or decision when route choice is premature
No persisted spec artifact by default
No execute runtime in route command
No approval bypass or wording hack guidance
May include /goal recommendation when host supports it

evals.name: reflect-skill-materialize-explicit-only
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect skill materialization mode. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should state that skill source generation is handled inside /tk:reflect only when the user explicitly targets skill and explicitly applies. It should use same-session same-ledger candidate_id or explicit description, require name confirmation before write, write to the agent-supported user skill surface (Claude Code example: ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md), and must not treat skill as a default-apply target.
expectations: uses same-session same-ledger candidate_id or explicit description
writes to agent-supported user skill surface

evals.name: arch-review-is-report-only-architecture-surface
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:arch-review behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should treat /tk:arch-review as an evidence-first, report-only architecture review surface. It should look for boundary leaks, ownership confusion, coupling hotspots, and repeated pain without claiming an auto-refactor happened. It must not mutate source, auto-generate docs as completion proof, or present architecture polish as implemented work. It should end with the smallest concrete next step rather than a vague rewrite slogan.
expectations: Looks for boundary ownership coupling repeated pain
Ends with one concrete next step

evals.name: ui-diff-bootstrap-missing-profile-instead-of-stop
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:ui-diff missing-profile behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should treat /tk:ui-diff as a direct QA surface that reads the current repo's `.claude/ui-diff/` profile. If the profile is missing, it should bootstrap repo-local missing files from bundled templates instead of stopping immediately. It should create only missing files, support a preview-only `--print-profile-template` mode, keep `login.local.md` as a gitignored local override, and avoid claiming engine install/update or user-global provisioning.
expectations: Uses bundled templates as bootstrap source

evals.name: learn-is-skill-only-source-to-skill-surface
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:learn behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should treat /tk:learn as a source-to-skill surface for path, directory, URL, current conversation, notes, or reflect candidate inputs. It should default to preview-only, require explicit apply before writing a skill source, keep a skill-only write boundary, avoid repo-local or user-global guidance writes, and use reflect ledger candidate data as the source of truth when invoked from reflect.
expectations: Does not route repo-local or user-global writes
Reads reflect ledger as source of truth
